---
title: Dhafer Malouche
---


<link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css" type="text/css">



<img src="IMG-1525.png" style="float: right; width:20%; border:1px solid; margin-right: 20px" align="left">


  
# {.tabset .tabset-fade .tabset-pills}

## About me

I’m Dhafer Malouche, an Associate Research Scholar at the Department of [Statistics and Data Science](https://statistics.yale.edu) at [Yale University](https://www.yale.edu). I also am affiliated with the [Whitney and Betty MacMillan Center for International and Area Studies](https://macmillan.yale.edu). Currently, I have been working on the [G-Econ](https://gecon.yale.edu) research project in the [Department of Economics](https://economics.yale.edu). Lead by Professor William Nordhaus, this project is devoted to developing a geophysically-based data set on economic activity for the world.  I'm also a Professor of Statistics on leave from the [University of Carthage](http://www.ucar.rnu.tn/Fr/) in Tunisia. 


For my everyday work, I use R, Python, H2O, and Spark. In this website I will be sharing many R and Python codes where I show how one can use these softwares to solve Machine Learning, Statistical Modeling, and Data Visualization problems. I would greatly appreciate all feedback, comments, and questions. 

So feel free to [contact me](mailto:dhafer.malouche@yale.edu)
